page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.32xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 7,600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with the Passmark CPU score, extended instructions, and encryption tests all placing in the top tier. Single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only large block operations scoring in the top 25%. The instance also demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. However, large-scale LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

Given its high resource density, integrated high-capacity NVMe storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, the m6idn.32xlarge offers a balanced cost-to-performance profile for data-intensive workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, data integration tasks, and multi-threaded enterprise applications. The absence of hardware acceleration makes it less suitable for heavy deep learning training or large LLM inference, but it remains highly efficient for general-purpose compute and high-speed storage operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.32xlarge is a general-purpose, Nitro-based instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 512 GB of DDR4 memory, 7,600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in Passmark CPU, extended instructions, and encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core processing and database operations. While single-core performance is average and large LLM inference is weak due to the lack of a GPU, the instance excels in memory bandwidth and static web serving. This hardware profile is highly suited for high-throughput databases, enterprise applications, and network-intensive workloads requiring substantial local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, 7,600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for databases and high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.32xlarge features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, 7.6 TB NVMe SSD, and 200 Gbps network speed for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-bandwidth general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7.6 TB of local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
512 GB of DDR4 memory and 7,600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and Nitro hypervisor integration
Top-tier performance in CPU encryption and extended instruction benchmarks
Optimized for high-throughput databases, web serving, and enterprise applications
